title: White House Cherry-Picks 3 Deflation Data Points While Energy Surges 44%
narrative: White House spokesperson Kush Desai stated: "The American economy remains on a solid trajectory... March CPI showed cooling core inflation and prices of beef, dairy, eggs, and prescription drugs actually declining thanks to the President's policies." Trump predicted on May 12 that inflation would drop as soon as the Iran war ends. The public narrative: the war's economic damage is contained and temporary, with the administration crediting its own policies for cooling prices.
telemetry: Brent crude up 44% since the war began; now trading at ~$105/barrel
CPI reached 3.3% — highest since May 2024; PCE projected at 4% by year-end (double the Fed's target)
Cherry-picked items (beef, dairy, eggs, Rx drugs) make up less than 8% of core CPI weighting
Energy prices — which surged 44% on crude — are weighted at ~8% but excluded from core inflation metrics by administration cherry-pickers
The three cherry-picked items all declined BEFORE the Iran war escalation, due to seasonal supply factors
Gas at $4+/gallon hits working-class Americans directly; the White House narrative focuses on grocery categories that barely register in household budgets at scale
analysis: The White House isn't lying about beef and egg prices — they're declining. What they're doing is selecting three categories that were already moving in their favor, ignoring the categories that are moving decisively against working Americans, and then attributing the decline to presidential policy. Energy costs are the dominant household inflation driver. Crude is up 44%. Gas is at $4.06 and climbing. Cherry-picking three grocery categories while energy prices surge is not an economic message — it's a narrative management operation.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: The White House cherry-picked three grocery categories to manufacture an inflation victory while energy prices — which hit every American at the pump, in heating bills, and in transport costs — surged 44%. CPI at 3.3% and climbing, PCE projected at 4% double the Fed's target. The 'solid trajectory' claim is the opposite of what the data shows. The war economy is delivering higher inflation, not lower.
